entry.title: Re: Make Company Code Settings - Asset Accounting-Specific
entry.content:
Dear @arul_selvan191,

In Public Cloud FY2026 cannot be opened in Asset Accounting while FY2024 is still open. SAP states that only two fiscal years can be open at the same time in Asset Accounting. As a result to open FY2026, FY2024 must first be closed

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/3e5fcf2c768746049b5627bd5a42f720/290e97c27ba3416cb7254cabe4d5dec4.html

This is standard system behavior, not a configuration defect. SAP documents Make Company Code Settings Asset Accounting-Specific (F7312) as the app used for year-end closing for individual depreciation areas. Therefore for a productive company code, FY2024 must be closed before FY2026 can be opened. If depreciation has been managed outside SAP the only SAP-supported alternative is a legacy data transfer / migration scenario. SAP documents that depreciation already posted in the legacy system can be transferred into SAP instead of being newly posted there. However, SAP’s migration guidance is based on the last closed fiscal year and does not support keeping FY2024 open while opening FY2026 in normal operations

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/3e5fcf2c768746049b5627bd5a42f720/cb476007e11c4221b22d87b80ed18c6d.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/3e5fcf2c768746049b5627bd5a42f720/1fd2035773b15632e10000000a441470.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/3e5fcf2c768746049b5627bd5a42f720/a185c3d89dae4ffc85723f27f624944a.html

Summary:

The system message is consistent with SAP standard. For a productive Asset Accounting company code, FY2024 must be closed first external depreciation handling changes the design only in a legacy transfer scenario and does not remove the fiscal-year opening control

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: How to get cart item's grouped line number with a script?
entry.content:
When the "Roll-Up Cart Items" AppParam is enabled, cart line item numbers are displayed as nested.

For example, if items 1, 4, 6 in the below list are main products,
1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6
then enabling that AppParam changes them to:
1, 1.1, 1.2, 2, 2.1, 3

But the script Quote.MainItems.Item.Rank returns the flat line item number.
What script can I use to get the nested format?

entry.title: System Conversion to SAP S/4HANA: Downtime Reduction with Technical Options
entry.content:
Document Teaser 

Downtime is always a topic in most SAP S/4HANA conversion projects. From technical point of view, what can we do to reduce and optimize the downtime? Let me share my experiences with this topic as part of my work in the SAP S/4HANA Customer Care and RIG team. 

1.   SAP S/4HANA Overview of Transition scenarios 

The transition to SAP S/4HANA is a strategic business transformation that involves moving from legacy systems like SAP ECC to SAP's modern, in-memory ERP platform. This transition is essential since SAP is planning to end standard support for SAP ECC in December 2027, with extended maintenance available until 2030. 

You may know the SAP S/4HANA overview of transition scenarios below. This blog will focus on SAP S/4HANA conversion scenarios only. 

2.   SAP S/4HANA Standard Conversion General Steps in downtime windows 

In a standard SAP S/4HANA conversion, the business downtime window is the period from when the system is unavailable for end users until it will become available for them again. 

Let’s look at the following general phases: 

  • System Ramp Down and Isolation: The process begins mainly with locking all users and stopping interfaces from/to external systems to ensure that no new data will be entered. Those are rather customer-specific steps, please always have your conversion delivery team align in detail with the customer IT and business teams. 
  • Software Update Manager (SUM) Downtime: SUM finalizes the conversion of SAP S/4HANA and switches the system to the new repository, this is the main part from technical point of view which we can optimize to reduce the total downtime. 
  • Finance (FI) Data Conversion: SUM converts FI table contents from the old SAP ERP data model to the new SAP S/4HANA data model. We treat it as a candidate for optimization, but we will not go into detail about it in this blog. 
  • SAP S/4HANA Technical and Application post-processing steps: Those steps are critical to finalize the system to get it ready again for business operations. These steps ensure that all functional requirements are covered with the new S/4HANA data models and that the system's technical infrastructure is stable. .System Validation and Ramp-up: Technical teams and key functional users verify core configurations and critical processes before releasing the system back to the end users. 

3.    Terminology and Approaches 

3.1   SAP S/4HANA Conversion Tools and Approaches (Conversion only) 

Approaches 

Scenarios and how to reduce the downtime 

Standard SUM/DMO 

Software Update Manager/Database Migration Option - Conversion with DB migration 

Depending on whether the source system is on an SAP HANA database or not, we will choose to use SUM or DMO by picking up different endpoints to launch for the conversion. There are general recommendations to work on to reduce the technical tool downtime periods. 

doDMO (Downtime Optimized Approach) 

Downtime-optimized Database Migration Option - Conversion with DB migration and downtime reduction (DB migration partly during uptime) 

doC (Downtime Optimized Approach) 

Downtime-optimized Conversion - Conversion with significant downtime reduction (DB migration partly and data conversion partly during uptime) 

DMO with System Move 

Conversion with DB migration and data center relocation 

DMOVE2S4  

DMO Move to SAP S/4HANA - Conversion with DB migration and data center relocation 

DMOVE2S4 with doDMO 

Combining DMOVE2S4 and doDMO – DC move, S/4HANA Conversion and reduce downtime by DB migration partly during uptime 

DMOVE2S4 with doC 

Combine DMOVE2S4 and doC  – DC move, S/4HANA Conversion and reduce downtime by DB migration and data conversion partly during uptime 

System Transition Workbench 

A guided tool for moving to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition includeing different scenarios 

NZDM 

near-Zero Downtime Maintenance (ABAP)  - Conversion from SAP Business Suite on HANA to SAP S/4HANA,  an advanced preconfiguration mode and feature in SUM with Record and Replay technique to reduce downtime   

NZDT 

Near-Zero Downtime Technology 

3.2 How to choose the most suitable conversion strategy and conversion path  

Scenario 

Source System 

Available Options 

On-Premise In-Place Conversion 

 

SAP ERP 6.0 on non-HANA Database 

DMO, doDMO, doC 

 

SAP ERP 6.0 on HANA Database 

SUM, nZDM, doDMO, doC 

Migration & Conversion to Hyperscalers / Customer's Other Data Center 

SAP ERP 6.0 on non-HANA Database 

DMO with System Move, DMOVE2S4, DMOVE2S4 with doDMO, DMOVE2S4 with doC 

 

SAP ERP 6.0 on HANA Database 

DMO with System Move, DMOVE2S4, DMOVE2S4 with doDMO, DMOVE2S4 with doC 

Migration & Conversion to SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud 

 

SAP ERP 6.0 on non-HANA Database 

DMO with System Move, DMOVE2S4, DMOVE2S4 with doDMO, DMOVE2S4 with doC, System Transition Workbench 

 

SAP ERP 6.0 on HANA Database 

DMO with System Move, DMOVE2S4, DMOVE2S4 with doDMO, DMOVE2S4 with doC, System Transition Workbench 

What are the factors and criteria for the decision making with different options?  

From technical point of view, it is not a complicated question, see below decision tree. 

At the same time, you can refer to Road Map Viewer - SAP for Me for the general information 

From overall project perspective, there are some important factors such as: 

  • Total project timeline and cost; 
  • Downtime reduction; 
  • Technical prerequisites and functional prerequisites; 
  • Etc. and customer can define by themselves. Here is a delivery example about the evaluation of the approach and tools options with pros and cons based on customer’s requirements and SAP shared the proposal in a high-level picture. 

If still you have doubts, please reach out to your SAP professional consulting and service teams about additional recommendations for the different approaches based on the system data, sizing report, readiness reports and so onSome customers we worked with choose in-place SAP S/4HANA conversion in their data center and migration of SAP S/4HANA with Copy to target SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. If you choose this option, please consider the total business downtime. And right now, SAP provides this with featured option: RISE with System Transition Workbench with scenario: SUM/DMO run in local data center and use HANA tenant copy to achieve this. 

 3.3   RISE with SAP System Transition Workbench  

It provides a pre-planned “route” tailored to your starting point and destination, saving you the time to plan and reach the route. It gives real-time traffic updates and suggests alternative routes, helping you avoid congestion and delays. 

About "RISE with SAP system transition workbench" | SAP Help Portal  

SAP System Transition Workbench is installed by Enterprise Cloud Services(ECS) on a VM Migration Server. You can get seamless support from your SAP delivery team and SAP development teams as it is released and generally available to all customers. 

There is one great introduction blog for this tool from Kristin. 

Introducing the RISE with SAP System Transition Wo... - SAP CommunityThe tool can be used for migration of customer systems to SAP Enterprise Cloud Services (ECS) in context of RISE with SAP. 

  • SAP HANA Tenant Copy 
  • SAP HANA Backup and Recovery 
  • Homogeneous "Update and Copy" 
  • System Conversion using SUM 2.0 (DMO with System Move) 
  • System Conversion using SUM 2.0 (DMOVE2S4) 

Usage Restriction 

  • The above mentioned scenario is permitted for SAP ECS internal operations as well as for qualified migration partners. 
    Qualified migration partners must have earned at least the “Essential Level” designation for the SAP Cloud ERP Private competency as visible in SAP Partner Finder. 
  • SAP provides required automation tooling and configuration of the virtual machine for the migration.  
    Any non-authorized software export of the automation tooling or its configuration is prohibited. 
    Any usage beyond defined usage constraints is not allowed. 
  • The RISE with SAP System Transition Workbench assumes that the target system and component releases are within SAP maintenance. Technical limitations and/or (general) restrictions might require additional preparation or an upgrade to newer releases of SAP S/4HANA related applications, add-ons, or other technical components. 
  • Currently, the RISE with SAP system transition workbench does not support SAP HANA scale-out target systems 

There is also the published success story of RISE with System Transition workbench helping from Piyush and Praveen to customer accelerated on their cloud journey to RISE.   

Accelerating RISE Migrations: CommScope’s Success Story with Transition Workbench Update and Copy - An Innovative migration approach for Rise 

3.4   Near-Zero Downtime Technology 

NZDT and doC are the two approaches as part of Minimized Downtime Services supporting all scenarios.  

As per SAP Note 693168 - Minimized Downtime Service (MDS) , doC is generally available to customers while NZDT is service-based and SAP Global Delivery Hub would have to be involved. Please always reach out to your SAP consulting team about NZDT scenarios since only a  limited number of customers can be supported. 

4.   Second level considerations and SUM/DMO optimizations 

4.1    Technical Downtime Optimization (TDO) app  

 To ensure that the maintenance using Software Update Manager on the production system goes smoothly, test cycles are performed before updating  production. And typically, the updated system is used to validate the upgrade process and identify any necessary preparation steps and fixes. 

To address these concerns, SAP offer the Technical Downtime Optimization App. This app provides multiple features for analyzing the Software Update Manager runtime and improving the maintenance event. 

 

You can refer to more details about the TDO usage: https://tdo.cloud.sap 

SAP Blog : Simply analyzing and improving maintenance events with Technical Downtime Optimization App 

4.2   General Recommendations to reduce technical downtime 

SAP Note 2351294 - S/4HANA System Conversion / Upgrade: Measures to reduce technical downtime 

4.3   General Recommendations on SAP HANA Database 

  • SAP HANA parameter changes will help data conversion performance but may impact productive performance. Therefore, if you apply these changes before entering the technical downtime, don’t forget to revert those changes after the completion of SUM. 
  • Increase the CPU capacity used for delta merges by changing the indexserver.ini parameter [mergedog] -> load_balancing_func value to “LCC / 2”. Remember the original value to restore it later. 
  • Increase the number of merge tokens and merge threads by setting indexserver.ini parameters [mergedog] -> token_per_table and [indexing] -> parallel_merge_threads to a value of available physical CPU cores divided by 2 (but no more than 256). 
  • Increase the parallelism for partitioned tables by setting indexserver.ini parameter [indexing] -> parallel_merge_part_threads to a value of available physical CPU cores divided by 2 (but no more than 256). If the parameter is not yet available in section [indexing], add it as a new parameter. 
  • Please monitor the memory usage of the HANA database server via visual load history of the HANA cockpit, or the relevant monitoring during the execution of critical phases. 

4.4   General recommendations on SUM/DMO parameters 

Setting up parallel processes properly can dramatically increase the performance of upgrades and updates of SAP systems. As best practice, the following should be considered: 

  • SAP Note 1616401 - Parallelism in the Upgrades, EhPs and Support Packages implementations 
  • Hardware resource utilization during Upgrades and Updates 

              SUM settings 1: ABAP PROCESSES (UPTIME) and ABAP PROCESSES (DOWNTIME) 

Number of parallel background processes and activator processes, also number of batch processes scheduled on the application server. The minimum number is 5. We recommend a value that is high enough to enable parallel processing, but small enough that the production system is not affected. 

              SUM settings 2: SQL PROCESSES (UPTIME) and SQL PROCESES (DOWNTIME) 

Number of parallel SQL processes used by tp to execute database statements. 

              SUM settings 3: R3TRANS PROCESSES (UPTIME) and R3TRANS PROCESSES (DOWNTIME) 

Number of parallel import processes used by R3trans to import data into the database. Note that these processes can generate significant loads on the database. 

              SUM settings 4: R3LOAD PROCESSES 

Number of R3load processes used for the EU_IMPORT phases. 

 

PHASES (SUM)

PARALLELISM MECHANISM

ACT_UPG

BATCH

DDIC_UPG

R3TRANS

PARCONV_UPG

SQL DDL

PARDIST_SHD

BATCH

SHADOW_IMPORT_INC

R3TRANS

SHADOW_IMPORT_UPG1

R3TRANS

SHADOW_IMPORT_UPG2

R3TRANS

TABIM_UPG

R3TRANS

XPRAS_AIMMRG

BATCH

EU_CLONE_MIG_DT/UT_RUN

R3load

 

4.5  The classic optimization methods 

  • Usually optimization is started with analysis of files SAPupConsole.log, LONGPOST LOG, UPGANA.XML and APPLANA.XML. 
  • DMO optimization: Benchmark tool for export/import: see blog Optimizing DMO Performance - SAP Community. 
  • Parallelization framework (SHDB_PFW_CONF) - based on hardware resources of the application server. 
  • SAP Note 2353814 - SAP S/4HANA conversion reports in SD: optimize parallel execution of conversion reports: . 
  • For AnyDB to SAP S/4HANA conversion: 
  • Duration files can be re-used and R3load migration logs are analyzed to check if better splitting can be determined, or a different order of packages. 
  • Source database statistics updates and, in some cases index rebuild as well as table reorganization can reduce the runtime. 

4.6    The steps and phases that contribute most during SUM/DMO downtime: 

  

Phase #  

Key Technical Steps 

Description 

1 

EU_CLONE_MIG_DT_EXP / IMP 

Export and Import: Application tables are migrated from the source database to the target HANA database. 

2 

PARCONV_UPG/ 

PARMVNT_APP_VIEWS 

Parallel Conversion: Converts application tables to the new release's structure and activates their nametabs. 

3 

TABIM_UPG 

Main Table Import: Imports remaining data into SAP-standard tables (classes S, W, E, C, G). 

4 

XPRAS_AIMMRG 

Execution of XPRAs: Runs Reports After Import (XPRAs) to adjust data and settings for the new system version. - Data conversion 

5 

JOB_KONV_MIGRATION/ 

RUN_VBFA_MIGRATION 

SFW - Shadow field Migration for KONV-PRCD_ELEMENTS Conversion 

6 

PARRUN_MKPF*_S4 / 

PARRUN_MKPF_VERIFY 

MM-IM conversion 

7 

PARRUN_FILL_EXTRACT_S4 

MM-IM Conversion 

8 

XPRAS_AIMMRG 

Execution of XPRAs: Runs Reports After Import (XPRAs) to adjust data and settings for the new system version. 

9 

Customer-specific XPRAs 

TABIM_UPG_CUST 

XPRAS_UPG_CUST 

Customer-specific XPRAs 

10 

RUN_SDMI_POST_PROCESSING 

Silent Data Migration: A post-processing step that migrates application data in the background after the system is back online, reducing technical downtime. 

4.7   PFW (SHDB Parallelization Framework) settings based on hardware resources of the App server 

SHDB Parallelization Framework  is and ABAP SAP_BASIS framework to simplify the parallelization topic for ABAP applications via PFW instances covering downtime conversion steps.              

The configuration table SHDB_PFW_CONF  

Report/ t_code:: SHDB_PDW_SUPPORT to configure and adjust the parameters or via scripts directly from sap note 2351294 - S/4HANA System Conversion / Upgrade: Measures to reduce technical downtime 

If still you have doubts, please reach out to your SAP professional consulting and service teams about additional recommendations for the different approaches based on the system data, sizing report, readiness reports and so on. 

5. Summary and Conclusion 

Optimizing downtime for an SAP S/4HANA conversion is not just about choosing the right tool; it is about a multi-layered approach that combines strategy, infrastructure tuning, and precise tool configuration. 

By selecting the most appropriate transition scenario (Standard, doDMO, or doC), leveraging the Technical Downtime Optimization (TDO) app, and fine-tuning parallelism within SUM and the HANA database, you can significantly shrink the business impact of your cutover. 

Key Takeaways: 

  • Analyze Early: Use the TDO app and previous mock run logs to identify bottlenecks. 
  • Tune the Engine: Don't settle for default SUM parameters; adjust ABAP and SQL processes based on your hardware. 
  • Leverage Modern Tools: Consider and compare the tools accelerate the journey. 

Ultimately, every system is unique. We highly recommend performing multiple test conversions to validate these optimizations and ensure a smooth, predictable go-live. 

If you have other questions / feedback, please let me know in the comments. 

Brought to you by the SAP S/4HANA Customer Care and RIG team.

entry.title: Re: SAP Agentic AI in Practice: Building the SAP Migration Assessment Agent (Part 2 of 6)
entry.content: @atul_joshi85 Thank you, that's exactly the balance I was aiming for. Clean core and AI aren't in tension when the agent operates as an assessment and recommendation layer rather than touching the core directly. The agent queries, analyzes, and surfaces findings — the human and the governed process own the action. That separation is what makes it both safe and practical for production SAP landscapes. Glad it resonated!

entry.title: Re: How to delete all private bookmarks in SAC Planning S...
entry.content:
Hi @PM8 

Before you move your stories in SAC, just head to the Bookmarks tab in the Stories area to clear things out. It’s the easiest way to delete them all in one spot!

One thing to keep in mind: if you're an admin, you can easily toss the Global (public) bookmarks, but the Personal (private) ones stay hidden—only the person who made them can delete those.

Check out:  How to Create and Use Bookmarks in Stories: SAP Analytics Cloud

Thanks,
Tuncay

entry.title: Connect SuccessFactors through Oauth using Postman
entry.content:
Step 1: Registering the OAuth2 Client Application

  1. In SuccessFactors, go to Manage OAuth2 Client Applications.

  2. Register a new application and generate a self-signed certificate.

  3. Important: Download the certificate to obtain your Private Key. You will also need the API Key (Client ID) generated by the system.

 

Step 2: Requesting the SAML Assertion

Before getting the final token, you must request the SAML Assertion string. This is where many developers get stuck.

  • Method: POST

  • URL: https://<api-server>/oauth/idp

  • Body (x-www-form-urlencoded):

KeyValue
client_id<Your_API_Key>
company_id<Your_Company_ID>
user_id<Technical_User_ID>
token_urlhttps://<api-server>/oauth/token
private_key<Your_Downloaded_Private_Key>
grant_typeurn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:saml2-bearer

The response will be a long Base64 string. This string is your Assertion.

Step 3: Exchanging the Assertion for the Access Token

Now, take the string received in Step 2 and use it in a second call:

  • Method: POST

  • URL: https://<api-server>/oauth/token

  • Body (x-www-form-urlencoded):

    • client_id: <Your_API_Key>

    • company_id: <Your_Company_ID>

    • grant_type: urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:saml2-bearer

    • assertion: <The_String_From_Step_2>

 

 

Step 4: Troubleshooting – The "IP Restriction" Pitfall

If you receive an error even with a valid assertion, the issue is likely the IP Whitelist. SuccessFactors enforces strict security for API users.

  1. Navigate to Password & Login Policy Settings.

  2. Click on Set API login exceptions...

  3. Add your user and ensure the IP address restrictions include your current outbound IP. Without this, the system will reject the OAuth handshake despite having correct credentials.

 

Conclusion

By following these steps—specifically the two-step POST process for the assertion and token—you can effectively test any SuccessFactors OData entity.

Tip: Remember that the access_token has a limited TTL (Time To Live). For automated integrations, ensure your logic handles token renewal gracefully.

Have you encountered any specific issues with the /oauth/idp endpoint in different Data Centers? Let's discuss below!

#SAPSuccessFactors #OData #OAuth2 #Postman #SAPBTP #APIIntegration

entry.title: Physical Inventory through WM in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud – Real-Time Implementation
entry.content:
Physical Inventory through WM in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud

In one of our recent projects, I worked on implementing the Warehouse Management process in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud. As part of this, I was responsible for configuring and testing the Physical Inventory (PI) process through WM. As part of this, I configured and executed the Physical Inventory process through WM. The complete PI cycle was tested end-to-end using the standard SAP process, ensuring that the system stock accurately reflects the actual physical stock in the warehouse.

Below, we will walk through the configuration setup and the practical execution of the process.

Step 1: Activity Area Configuration

Menu Path:

Manage Your Solution -> Configuration -> Logistics, Transportation and Warehousing -> Warehouse Management -> Warehouse Master Data -> Activity Area

Here, I used the standard Activity Area “INVE”, and the Warehouse Process Category is maintained as “4 – PI”.

Define Activity Area: I created new Activity Area and assigned to the respective warehouse.

Assign Storage Bins to Activity Areas: In this step, the created Activity Area is assigned to the respective Storage Type. By doing this, all the storage bins under that storage type are linked to the Activity Area, which allows the system to process Physical Inventory activities for those bins.

Define Sort Sequence for Activity Areas: In this step, I assigned the Activity Area along with the relevant Activity and Storage Type. This configuration helps the system decide the sequence in which storage bins are picked during the Physical Inventory process, so that the counting is carried out in an organized way.

Step 2: Define Physical Inventory Area

Menu Path:

Warehouse Management -> Internal Warehouse Processes -> Physical Inventory in the Warehouse

Define Physical Inventory Area: In this step, I defined the Physical Inventory Area by assigning the Warehouse Number along with the relevant Activity Area. Additionally, the required Physical Inventory process indicators are maintained, which control how the counting and posting process is executed within the warehouse.

Define Number Range for Physical Inventory Documents: The number range for Physical Inventory documents is maintained.

Specify Physical-Inventory-Specific Settings in the Warehouse: In this step, the Physical Inventory specific settings are maintained at the warehouse level.

Key details such as the Fiscal Year Variant and Number Range for PI documents are configured based on business requirements.

Define Tolerance Group for Difference Analyzer: In this step, I defined the Tolerance Group and assigned it to the Warehouse. The tolerance limits are maintained based on business requirements, which control how inventory differences are handled during the Physical Inventory process.

For testing purposes, I have set it as “No Tolerance”, so that the system considers the full difference without any limits.

Define Tolerance Group for Posting Differences: I defined the Tolerance Group for Posting Differences.

If the difference exceeds the maintained tolerance, the system can restrict or control the posting process.

For testing purposes, the “No Tolerance” option is maintained, so all differences are considered without any restriction.

As shown above, the system throws an error stating that the document item cannot be posted due to exceeded tolerances.

This behavior is controlled by the tolerance settings maintained in the configuration. If the difference goes beyond the defined limit, the system restricts the posting of the Physical Inventory document. This helps ensure proper validation and control over inventory adjustments.

Define Tolerance Group for Recounting: I defined the Tolerance Group for Recounting.

For testing purposes, the “No Tolerance” option is maintained, so recount is not restricted by any limits.

Assign Tolerance Group - Count Confirmation App: I mapped the Tolerance Group to the Warehouse User (Warehouse Person).

Assign Tolerance Group- Differences App:

Define Priorities: Standard configuration is used, where priority levels like Very High, High, Medium, and Low are already defined.

Define Standard Reason for Each Phy. Inv. Procedure: Each Physical Inventory procedure (such as Annual, Cycle Counting, or Ad-hoc) is linked with a specific reason code. This helps in identifying the purpose of the Physical Inventory process and improves tracking and reporting, I maintained STD.

Define Print Form for Physical Inventory Documents: The required fields to be printed on the Physical Inventory document are selected based on business needs. This ensures that the printed document contains all necessary details for warehouse users during the counting process.

Step 3: Output Parameter Determination

Ensure that the Warehouse Number is correctly assigned to the respective WM Physical Inventory process in the output determination. The appropriate print queue (printer settings) should also be maintained so that Physical Inventory documents can be printed as required.

Step 4: Define Search Sequence of Creation Rules for Activity Areas:

Menu path: Warehouse Management-> Warehouse Cross Processes-> Warehouse Order-> Define Search Sequence of Creation Rules for Activity Areas

In this step, I assigned the Warehouse Number, Activity Area, and Activity along with the Warehouse Order creation rule. I maintained the standard WO creation rule (SR04 – WO for Physical Inventory without limit) to ensure proper grouping of warehouse tasks during the Physical Inventory process.

 

Testing the Physical Inventory Process

Now, we proceed to test the Physical Inventory process flow for product-specific (AS) using the standard WM process. There are several Physical Inventory counting procedures available, and for testing purposes, I am using “AS – Annual Physical Inventory (Product-Specific)”

Step 1: Create Physical Inventory Document

App: Create Inventory Documents – Physical Inventory

The Physical Inventory document is created for a product-specific “AS” scenario.

Here, I selected the product “FG Material” with batch “0000000454” for the Physical Inventory count.

Before performing the Physical Inventory count, I verified the current stock available in the warehouse.

Verified at the IM stock level.

I selected the required material “FG Material” along with batch “0000000454” and created the Physical Inventory document using the Create Inventory Documents – Physical Inventory app.

Step 2: Count Physical Inventory - Paper-Driven Counting

Once the PI document is created, the system automatically generates a Warehouse Order (WO) in the background for the counting process.

In a paper-driven process, the PI document can be printed using this app and provided to the warehouse person for physical counting.

Step 3: Counting (Enter Physical Count)

Once the warehouse person completes the physical counting based on the printed document, the count needs to be entered into the system using the same app.

Here, the system stock was 100 Qty. For testing, I reduced 1 quantity from the respective Handling Unit (301742) to simulate a difference scenario. After entering the updated count, I confirmed the count in the system, which resulted in a stock difference.

Step 4: Post the Physical Inventory Document

After confirming the count, I proceeded to post the Physical Inventory document. I selected the PI document and executed “Post Physical Inventory Document”.

Since I reduced the count by 1 quantity, once the Physical Inventory document was posted, the system automatically identified the stock difference.

The difference can be posted, which updates the stock and completes the adjustment process in the system.

After posting the Physical Inventory differences, the stock can be verified again at both WM and IM levels. As shown, the stock is now reduced from 100 to 99, reflecting the Physical Inventory adjustment.

IM Stock level

Conclusion:

From this implementation, I have shown the Physical Inventory configuration through WM and the end-to-end PI process execution for product-based “AS” in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud. This helps ensure accurate stock tracking and better control over inventory differences in the warehouse.

There's usually a bit of a "domino effect" where the errors are linked together in a hierarchy. Try fixing that date length issue first, and you might find the rest of the errors disappear!

entry.title: Detailed Document Search - Not fetching the goal details with Latest Experience of Performance Forms
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

In our company, we use Detailed Document Search for audit purposes to download the performance review forms for terminated employees.

We tested Latest Experience of Performance Reviews and observed that the admins or HR person with access to Detailed Document Search tool is not able to view the Goal Details of the employee whenever they download the report using Detailed Document Search tool. This was not the case with the legacy experience of Performance Reviews.

We raised a incident with SF and they replied back stating that we need to add F role to the read access of the fields and also on the private-access option on the Goal Plan template. Once we added this, Admins were able to download the report using Detailed Document Search tool but the layout is not as per the legacy layout.

If this is the approach to follow, then we need to add F role to all the prior goal plan templates so that admins can download the reports using Detailed Document Search. But this approach needs more changes to the closed goal plan templates.

I want to know how the other customers are handling this issue with Latest Experience of Performance Review forms.

I raised an influence request to provide the same functionality of Detailed Document Search tool (like prior) with the Latest Experience of Performance Review forms. Appreciate if you can vote for the same.

https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/370907

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Crystal Enterprise SMTP SSL Negotiation
entry.content:
Found the issue eventually.

Check if the certificate is working via openSSL:

openssl s_client -starttls smtp -connect smtp.office365.com:587 -nocommands -CAfile "C:\Program Files (x86)\SAP BusinessObjects\SAP BusinessObjects Enterprise XI 4.0\win64_x64\certificate.crt"

If it returns this:

0C1F0000:error:05800088:x509 certificate routines:X509_load_cert_crl_file_ex:no certificate or crl found:crypto\x509\by_file.c:275:

Then make shure you unblock the new .crt file via Explorer --> Right mouse button on certificate.crt --> Properties --> Unblock.

Run the command again and you should see this (and alot more):

Connecting to 40.101.121.34
CONNECTED(00000204)
depth=2 C=US, O=DigiCert Inc, OU=www.digicert.com, CN=DigiCert Global Root G2
verify return:1
depth=1 C=US, O=DigiCert Inc, CN=DigiCert Global G2 TLS RSA SHA256 2020 CA1
verify return:1
depth=0 C=US, ST=Washington, L=Redmond, O=Microsoft Corporation, CN=outlook.com
verify return:1

Restart the Adaptive Job Server and reschedule, should now work properly!

entry.title: Still digging in — hope you will too.
entry.content:
In CAP, most things are automatic. You define an entity in service.cds and you get full CRUD for free — GET, POST, PATCH, DELETE, all handled by the framework without a single line of TypeScript.

Actions are different. An Action is custom business logic you define explicitly:

action PlaceOrder(productId: UUID, quantity: Integer) returns {
  orderId : UUID;
  message : String;
};

CAP doesn't know what to do with this. There's no default behavior. The only thing that makes it work is a handler in service.ts:

this.on("PlaceOrder", async (req) => {
  // validate stock, create order, update inventory
});

No handler → no behavior → HTTP 501.

Locally, this worked perfectly. On BTP, 501.

First theory: missing @impl

After some digging, I found that in production, CAP doesn't load .cds files directly. It loads a compiled csn.json — a pre-built representation of your model generated by cds build --production.

The issue: csn.json doesn't automatically know where your TypeScript implementation lives. You have to tell it explicitly with @impl:

// BEFORE — CAP doesn't find the implementation in production
service WarehouseService @(requires: 'WarehouseUser', path: '/warehouse') {

// AFTER — explicit pointer to the implementation file
service WarehouseService @(requires: 'WarehouseUser', path: '/warehouse', impl: 'srv/service') {

I added @impl. Deployed. Still 501.

The real problem: BTP doesn't run TypeScript

This is the part that took real time to understand.

CAP supports TypeScript officially — cds watch handles compilation transparently via ts-node. But on BTP, CAP runs as a standard Node.js app. No ts-node, no runtime translation. The compilation step you never saw locally becomes your responsibility.

Environment CAP loads Executes Result

Local (cds watch)service.tsts-node (live)works
BTP without fixservice.jsnot found501
BTP with fixservice.jsNode.js runs itworks

@impl was correct. But it was pointing to a file that didn't exist — because I'd never compiled TypeScript to JavaScript.

The fix — or at least the one I found

I added a tsconfig.srv.json that compiles only the service handlers, outputting to the exact path CAP expects on BTP:

{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "target": "ES2022",
    "module": "commonjs",
    "outDir": "./gen/srv/srv",
    "rootDir": "./srv"
  },
  "include": ["srv/**/*.ts"]
}

outDir: ./gen/srv/srv — this is where cds build --production puts everything else. CAP finds service.js there and loads it.

The deploy script now runs in sequence:

npm run deploy
# cds build --production        → compiles .cds → csn.json (with @impl)
# npx tsc -p tsconfig.srv.json  → compiles service.ts → gen/srv/srv/service.js
# mbt build + cf deploy         → packages and deploys to BTP

Two things needed to be true at the same time:

  • @impl in service.cds — tells CAP where to look
  • service.js in gen/srv/srv/ — what CAP actually finds there

Either one alone doesn't work. Both together do.

(There may be cleaner approaches — if you know one, I'd genuinely like to hear it in the comments.)

How to read the cf logs

Once I knew what to look for, the logs became useful:

# service.ts is NOT loaded:
serving WarehouseService { decl: 'srv/csn.json' }

# service.ts IS loaded correctly:
serving WarehouseService { impl: 'srv/service', decl: 'srv/csn.json' }

One word difference. Significant difference in behavior.

The source stays TypeScript

service.ts is still the source of truth. The compiled service.js is a build artifact — regenerated on every deploy, never edited directly.

The workflow is exactly like any TypeScript project targeting Node.js: write TS, compile to JS for deployment. CAP just requires you to wire up that step yourself.

Key takeaway

Local CAP hides a lot: ts-node running in the background, .cds files loaded directly, SQLite instead of HANA, mock auth instead of XSUAA. Each of these can produce different surprises in production.

On BTP, the rules are more explicit: compiled artifacts, real services, no runtime shortcuts. Not a criticism — just something worth knowing before you hit it.

The gap between local and production behavior isn't documented clearly in one place. Hopefully this saves someone a few hours of cf logs --recent.

What these two posts are really about

Two posts in, and the technical problems — 501 errors, compilation gaps, deployment scripts — are only half the story.

The other half is what becomes possible once those problems are solved.

A PlaceOrder action isn't just a handler. It's business logic running inside an enterprise-grade platform — with built-in authentication, audit trails, multi-tenancy, and a service mesh that connects to everything SAP touches. ERP systems, procurement workflows, supply chain data, financial records. The same OData endpoint that returns a list of products can, with the right authorization and the right integration, reach into systems managing billions in transactions.

That's not where I am today. I'm a few weeks in, debugging cf logs and writing tsconfig files. But I understand now why the layers exist — and what they're protecting.

For a business, this architecture isn't overhead. It's the foundation that lets you move fast at scale without rebuilding trust every time something changes. The keep-alive endpoint I built for a free trial instance is the same pattern, at a different order of magnitude, that SREs use to manage production SLAs. The @impl annotation that took me hours to debug is the same explicit contract that makes large teams able to work on the same service without stepping on each other.

I crossed the wall because something clicked during a demo. What I found on the other side wasn't a different kind of technology — it was the same ideas I already knew, applied at a different scale, with different stakes.

That gap is bridgeable. That's the point.

 The goal isn't to look like I have it figured out. The goal is to document the path honestly — including the dead ends — so the next developer crossing this wall has something to read. I'm also at the starting point...

Stack: SAP CAP · BTP Cloud Foundry · SAP HANA Cloud · Node.js · TypeScript · Next.js 16 · Vercel · ++

entry.title: Re: Question
entry.content:
Hi @Soumik_ABAP ,

Appending tables (append structures) allow customers to add custom fields to existing SAP standard tables without modifying the original table structure.
They provide a safe, upgrade-stable method for extending SAP tables with customer-specific fields while preserving modifications during system upgrades.

Here are some documents for your reference.
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_NETWEAVER_731_BW_ABAP/ec1c9c8191b74de98feb94001a95dd76/cf21eb61446011d189700000e8322d00.html?version=LATEST&locale=en-US&state=PRODUCTION

[ABAP Cloud] https://help.sap.com/docs/abap-cloud/abap-development-tools-user-guide/append-structures?version=LATEST&locale=en-US&state=PRODUCTION

Best regards,
Charles

entry.title: Re: Second handed VAT process in SAP Public Cloud Portugal
entry.content:
Good day @Marlene_Fitas 

Thank you for your question.

The Public Cloud can handle it, but not with a dedicated “second hand goods industry” process. However, it offers you the possibility to take the standard approach where you model the items as normal products and run them through standard purchase to stock and sales from stock process.

Here are references for more guidance

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/f86dc2eb1f8b48c880a7607213104b27/01aeec57f7f2f032e10000000a441470.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ERP/96bf9ad642cf4b26a29595e3d573fb8c/9d7cbd534f22b44ce10000000a174cb4.html?locale=en-US

If your business scenario also includes customer returns or reclassification of goods into used or second-hand stock, you may consider posting them into a specified target stock, such as the stock for second hand goods, through a Logistical Follow up Activities in returns processing.

As a next step in your process, you should:

  • Create the used goods as normal products in Manage Product Master Data.

  • Decide whether you need serial numbers for individual traceability. See Serialization Procedures for more details.
  • Run the standard purchase and sales process for these items.
  • If goods come back from customers and should be resold as used items, use Transfer to Specified Stock (0015) to move them into (second)-second-hand goods stock.

See the following references for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/aad8417242c84d70a64b2742fe818c90.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/ec5057f7b5d04f9f95231713116b9a98.html?locale=en-US

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

Data Integration in SAP Incentive Management Using SAP Smart Data Integration

entry.title: Re: YCOA G/L Account Automatic Posting Assignment Documen...
entry.content:
Good day @byd 

Thank you for your question.

There is no single official Public Cloud article that maps every YCOA G/L account to one automatic posting key a one business trigger. The better approach is for you to combine:

  1. The YCOA account catalog and account purpose texts
  2. The Automatic Account Determination configuration
  3. The process specific documentation for COGS split, inventory change and production variances.

Here are references for more guidance

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/32ed3c6435ea46afb4dbab6839fabe8e.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/s4hana-best-practices/ycoa-1f2ae10b96f740759d66e695f953aa8f/account-determination?locale=en-US&version=2025-FPS1

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/4032610758dc437089f0c28320eec93f/9ad11a34f07e4c8d92367bf03ab467d8.html?locale=en-US

For your examples:

  • 511/515/516/519 are standard consumption accounts and are tied to GBB/VBR goods issue.
  • 540xxx are mainly inventory change/reserve/source accounts, for example 54083000 is GBB/VAY goods issue for sales and a source for COGS split.

Here are references for more guiding details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/s4hana-best-practices/ycoa/54300000-inventory-change-semi-finished-goods?locale=en-US

  • 50301000 to 50309500 are COGS split target accounts which are mainly based on cost, not a simple one movement type accounts.
  • 520/525 are variance and price difference accounts whose trigger depends on the specific variance category in production accounting and valuation logic.
  • 55100000 is a production order adjustment/activity settlement account tied to AUA/AUF logic.

 

I suggest you:

  • Start with the YCO accelerator and the individual YCOA account pages.
  • In your tenant, check Automation Account Determination for the transaction keys and valuation classes.

  • Use Manage COGS Splits for 50301xxx to 50309xxx.

  • Use the Event Based WIP and Variance Posting documentation for 520xxx, 525xxx, and related production variance accounts.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Can MRP prioritize one BOM alternative component and...
entry.content:
Hello @FaisalMaayeh

Yes, your clarification is correct.

In Public Cloud, MRP stays on the planning logic of the preferred component, while the Process Order availability check is the point where the system can become aware and switch to the alternative component based on your BOM alternative item setup.

So, this does align with Option A:

  • Maintain the alternative item group in the BOM
  • Let execution handle the fallback during the order availability check.

I suggest you:

  1. Keep the alternative item group setup as you tested it.
  2. Confirm that component availability check is configured to run at the correct point, such as order creation or release.
  3. Accept that the planning signal may still remain focused on B, even if execution swaps to C.
  4. Check whether collective orders are in scope, because strategy 2 alternative item have a restriction there.

Here are references for more guidance

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c0c54048d35849128be8e872df5bea6d/fe4ce5e17e1d415db51294d0d855c162.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2bba750d1e124e1ea2a039bb1cd9b6c5/e9f1c0b7a068415b9359e3023d3cc9d6.html?locale=en-US

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: 1H 2026 - Release highlights of SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding
entry.content:
Hi Community,

The 1H 2026 release is here!

A new set of enhancements has been released in the 1H 2026 release that has been eagerly awaited. The SAP SuccessFactors 1H 2026 Release includes some key features and enhancements, so let's take a closer look!

1- Additional Onboarding Home Page Card :

Users assigned as buddies to a new hire now see a new SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding home page card featured at the top of their latest home page.

      Enablement: Customer Configured

      Configuration Requirements:

  • Ensure that the latest home page is enabled.

2- Automatic Enablement of Enhanced Offboarding Dashboard:

Users having access to the legacy Offboarding Dashboard are now automatically assigned with the required role-based permissions to migrate and enable them to use the enhanced Offboarding Dashboard.

For new users who are using the enhanced Offboarding Dashboard after 1H 2026, administrators must perform the following prerequisite checks before granting the required permissions to ensure that users can access the dashboard seamlessly:

  • Ensure that the Offboarding - requires "Enable Generic Objects", "Enable Business Process Engine", "Enable Intelligent Services”, and "Enable the Attachment Manager" switch is enabled from Provisioning>List of Companies>Company Settings.
  • Ensure that the user is granted the required Offboarding Process permission. For more information, see the Role-Based Permissions for Onboarding and Offboarding Tasks topic from the Related Information section.

Enablement: Automatically on

Role-Based Permission Prerequisites:

  • User Permissions>Onboarding or Offboarding Permission>Show Data Review Status on Offboarding Dashboard
  • User Permissions>Onboarding or Offboarding Permission>Show Offboarding Activity Status on Offboarding Dashboard
  • Onboarding or Offboarding Object Permission>Aggregated Details>View and Edit
  • Onboarding or Offboarding Permission>Process Responsible>View and Edit

3- Cancel Form I-9 and E-Verify Processes from the New Hire Details page:

Hiring managers and onboarding participants can now cancel Form I-9 and E-Verify processes using the Manage Compliance Processes button on the New Hire Details page.

The Manage Compliance Processes button appears at the top of the Form I-9 and E-Verify section of the New Hire Details page when there are any compliance processes. If there are no compliance process, the Manage Compliance Processes button is not visible.

When selected, the button opens the Compliance Process Details page. This page displays the new hire's compliance processes. The manager or participant can view the status of the process in the Status column. The Cancel Process button is available for Form I-9 and E-Verify processes.

Upon confirmation, the system marks the task as Canceled, records the timestamp, user, and reason, and logs the action in the audit trail. Canceled tasks are immediately removed from active dashboards, home page cards, and To-do tasks, but remain available in reporting and audit views with a clear Canceled status. All associated forms and attachments for the relevant section are discarded, and no further actions can be taken on the canceled task.

The View Details action on the Compliance Process Details page opens the Compliance Process Details popup. This page displays the Process ID, Start Date, and Comments.

Administrators and onboarding participants benefit from improved control over onboarding and compliance workflows, reduced support burden, and cleaner audit trails by being able to cancel unnecessary or erroneous Form I-9 and E-Verify tasks directly in the application.

Enablement: Automatically on

4- Change In Availability of Dependents Information:

Enabling the Country/Region-Specific Dependent Relationship Information setting in the latest People Profile removes the Dependents section from the SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding application.

Once this setting is enabled, the Dependents section isn't displayed for new hires and managers who use the Onboarding application. Enabling the Country/Region-Specific Dependent Relationship Information setting changes the system's behavior to manage employees' dependent information accordingly.

While new hires can't view the Dependents section while performing their personal data collection, managers can't view it while performing the following tasks:

  • New hire data review
  • Rehire data review
  • Accessing New Hire Data Review page
  • Final data review
  • Manage pending hires
  • Adding new hire to Onboarding

New hires and managers can't add new dependents or edit information specific to existing dependents while performing any of the tasks listed above. Additionally, once the Country/Region-Specific Dependent Relationship Information setting is enabled, any dependent data included in the Onboarding process is ignored during the SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding to SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central hire process that is enabled using the latest People Profile's hire process. Therefore, the system doesn't save the Dependents data available in the Onboarding application, but automatically aligns with the Country/Region-Specific Dependent Information available in Employee Central.

This enhancement ensures consistent handling of dependent information for administrators when the Country/Region-Specific Dependent Relationship Information setting is enabled.

Enablement: Customer Configured

Configuration Requirements:

  • Ensure that the latest People Profile is enabled.
  • Enable the Manage Employee Central Settings>Person, Employment, Worker Type>Country/Region-Specific Dependent Relationship Information setting.

5- Citizenship Status Change During Form I-9 Reverification:

As an administrator, you can now handle changes in an employee's citizenship or immigration status during Form I-9 Reverification. The system guides hiring managers through compliant workflows when employees transition from authorized noncitizen to Lawful Permanent Resident or U.S. citizen.

Enablement: Automatically on

6- Enhanced Hiring Status Indicator on Latest People Profile:

As an administrator, you can now identify and manage the profiles of the new hires with the introduction of the hiring status indicator on their profile.

The new hire's profile views are now enhanced to dynamically indicate the hiring status and allow administrators to access and manage their profiles accordingly. An updated Onboarding New Hire indicator, with supporting information text, is introduced to indicate the hiring status of the new hire. This indicator is removed once the onboarding process is completed and the new hire is converted to an employee.

Before MPH:

After MPH:

After Onboarding is cancelled:

After new hire is converted to employee:

Enablement: Automatically on

7- Enhanced Limit of Responsible Group Dropdown in Manage Onboarding and Offboarding Programs:

As an administrator, you can now access all available responsible groups in the Manage Onboarding and Offboarding Programs page.

You can now view and select all available responsible groups without any limitations while assigning them for tasks displayed in the Manage Onboarding and Offboarding Tasks>Onboarding Programs or Offboarding Programs, specifically from the Assigned to dropdown available in the Task Attributes dialog.

Enablement: Automatically on

8- Initiate the U.S Form I-9 Correction or Reverification Processes from the New Hire Details Page:

Human Resources administrators, managers, and onboarding participants can now initiate the Correct I-9 Section 1 or Correct I-9 Section 2 processes, and the Start Reverification process from the Action menu of the New Hire Details page. The Complete Form I-9 Section 3 task is now available in the Form I-9 and E-Verify tab on the Onboarding Dashboard.

The Action menu in the New Hire Details page now includes a new option to Initiate I-9 Process.

When selected, a popup appears, prompting the hiring manager or onboarding participant to Select an I-9 Process to Initiate.

Enablement: Customer configured

Configuration Requirements:

To ensure that Form I-9 Reverification task is assigned to the correct assignee, configure the responsible group and add people or roles to the group. The Reverification task uses the responsible groups as the Form I-9 signature task. For more information on how to configure the responsible group for the Reverification task see, Creating a Responsible Group for the Form I-9 Signature Task in the Related Information section.

Role-Based Permission Prerequisites:

Enable the Administrator Permissions>Manage Onboarding  I-9 Reverification permission.

9- New Task Management Page for Onboarding Tasks:

You can now access the onboarding specific tasks from the Task Management page, improving the task accessibility and management.

This new Task Management page is introduced within the Onboarding framework itself, to centralize the management of all onboarding tasks and reducing the dependency on the accessibility of the tasks only from the To-Do side panel.

You can access the Task Management page from the Manage Tasks button on the Onboarding Dashboard:

You can also create a custom quick link to the Task Management page on the latest home page.

Enablement: Customer configured

Configuration Requirements:

To enable the Task Management page, select Enable Task Management on Onboarding Dashboard under Admin Center>Onboarding General Settings.

10- Pre-Delivered Custom Task Template Objects:

As an administrator, you can now use pre-delivered custom task template objects to create custom tasks for Onboarding and Offboarding scenarios.

In addition to using standard MDF objects, you can now use pre-delivered custom task template objects to create onboarding and offboarding custom tasks. The template objects offer a curated set of optimized configurations that serve as a starting point for onboarding and offboarding custom task configuration.

The system naming convention for the pre-delivered template objects is ONB2CustomTaskData followed by three-digit numerical string ranging from 001 to 500, for example ONB2CustomTaskData001.

When you activate a custom task template, it becomes a custom task object and is no longer listed as an available template object. When this happens, the system automatically generates a replacement custom task template to maintain five template objects at all times, until you reach the 500-template object limit. The activated custom task template objects appear in a new section called Manage Custom Tasks located under the admin setting Manage Onboarding and Offboarding Tasks. All custom tasks created using the pre-delivered templates appear in this section along with any previously created custom tasks. You can edit existing custom tasks, or add a new custom task from the Manage Custom Tasks section.

Enablement: Customer Configured

Configuration Requirements:

Activate and configure the pre-delivered custom task templates from Admin Center>Configure Object Definitions. For more information, see Creating a Data Object for Custom Tasks in the Related Information section.

After activation, you can rename the custom task objects and customize field labels from Manage Onboarding and Offboarding Tasks>Manage Custom Tasks  <Task Name>  Edit Your Custom Task >Open Template Editor. For more information, see Template Editor Configuration in the Related Information section.

Role-Based Permission Prerequisites:

You need the Manage Permission Roles>MDF Foundation Objects>Configure Object Definitions permission to activate and configure template objects.

11- Responsible Group Limit Increased to 500 Users:

The Onboarding Responsible Group limit is now 500 users from 200 users.

Enablement: Automatically on

12- Restart e-Signature Step for Document Flows:

As an onboarding participant, you can now restart only the Complete e-Signature step when you make changes to the new-hire data. This ensures that the updated information is reflected accurately without restarting the entire onboarding process.

For each document flow, the Restart button is available in the View Details action window of its corresponding Complete e‑Signature task on the New Hire Details page.

Enablement: Customer configured

Role-Based Permission Prerequisites:

  • Ensure that you've enabled Admin Center>Manage Permission Roles>User Permissions>Onboarding or Offboarding Permissions>Restart Onboarding Step for E-Signature.

13- Review Growth Portfolio Task for Internal Hires:

As an administrator, you can now add a Review Growth Portfolio task to the onboarding checklist task for internal hires. This ensures that the internal hires review and update their growth portfolio for their upcoming role as part of the onboarding process itself.

After adding this task to the Onboarding program from Admin Center>Manage Onboarding and Offboarding Tasks.

The onboarding participants see the Review Growth Portfolio Task under Onboarding Dashboard>New Hire Details>New Hire Tasks.

The internal hires see the Review Growth Portfolio Task under Your Onboarding Tasks card appearing on their home page (both legacy and latest versions). Selecting the task navigates to the internal hire's Onboarding Checklist and the task is accessible under Additional Onboarding Tasks.

Enablement: Automatically on 

Configuration Requirements:

You've migrated to Talent Intelligence Hub and enabled Growth Portfolio.

Role-Based Permission Prerequisites:

To view the Review Growth Portfolio task on the Onboarding Journey page, ensure that both the onboarding participants and the internal hires have the View permission under Admin Center>Manage Permission Roles>User Permissions>Onboarding or Offboarding Admin Object>Permissions>ONB2GrowthPortfolioActivity.

14- Target Population Enforcement for Rehire Inactive Employee:

You can now enforce target population restrictions for Rehire Inactive Employee (by match in New Hire) and Rehire Inactive Employee with New Employment (by match in New Hire) permissions in Onboarding.

This update ensures that Onboarding respects the target population settings defined for the relevant Employee Central permissions when processing rehire matches, both for old and new employment.

Administrators can enable the new settings "Enable target population for rehire on old employment" and "Enable target population for rehire on new employment" in Manage Data.

Enablement: Customer configured

Role-Based Permission Prerequisites:

  • To allow the hiring manager to rehire an inactive employee with new employment by "Match" enable, go to Admin Center>Manager Permission Roles>Permissions>Manage Hires and enable the Rehire Inactive Employee with New Employment (by "Match" in New Hire) permission.
  • To allow the hiring manager to rehire an inactive employee on old employment by "Match" enable, Admin Center>Manager Permission Roles>Permissions>Manage Hires and enable the Rehire Inactive Employee (by "Match" in New Hire) permission.

entry.title: Re: Asset Accounting
entry.content: Hi NathanGenez, Thank you for your reply. I understand that instead of creating legacy assets and transferring values, it is better to update the account determination and post a journal entry. Could you please let me know if there is any specific T-code in SAP for handling this from the asset accounting side? I would appreciate it if you could elaborate on the process. If I follow this approach, will the planned depreciation values, APC, accumulated depreciation values in the asset continue to be calculated and updated in the same way as before?

entry.title: Re: Make Company Code Settings - Asset Accounting-Specific
entry.content:
Dear @arul_selvan191,

No. Setting the depreciation key to 0000 does not provide a supported way to bypass the fiscal-year opening control in Asset Accounting. SAP documents depreciation key 0000 No Depreciation and No Interest and SAP also states that a depreciation key can be assigned to an asset master record for a specific time interval. That controls depreciation calculation for the relevant asset/interval but it does not change the fiscal-year opening logic in Asset Accounting

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/47201366da8a460faad26dfe2c0b36a2/964a531a76c9454986471b7c4f508be3.html

 https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/3e5fcf2c768746049b5627bd5a42f720/aec7ecb2ee11437bbbc2041572be9612.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/47201366da8a460faad26dfe2c0b36a2/5d6917c0800e41df9d1df6a50ac99698.html

There is also no supported mass reversal of depreciation postings after the posting run. In the Public Cloud Asset Accounting FAQ, SAP explicitly states that depreciation postings cannot be reversed. At the same time SAP documents reversal functions for certain Asset Accounting journal entries which confirms that the limitation is specific to depreciation postings rather than to all asset postings in general

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/3e5fcf2c768746049b5627bd5a42f720/cb476007e11c4221b22d87b80ed18c6d.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/3e5fcf2c768746049b5627bd5a42f720/d2e8595c5ca74db486f194273dd9369c.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/3e5fcf2c768746049b5627bd5a42f720/f0a16409fbc649b896add04c1c0a624e.html

Summary:

Depreciation key 0000 can stop future depreciation calculation for the relevant asset interval, but it is not a workaround for the Asset Accounting year-end control. If depreciation has already been posted, SAP Public Cloud documents that these depreciation postings cannot be reversed, so a mass reversal option is not available

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Good to know: "SQL Statement Collection" the Swiss army knife for SAP HANA database administrators
entry.content:
• Why was this blog post created?
  • Where can I find the most important information about SQL Statement Collection reports?
  • Other articles
  • Do you have further questions?
  • Contribution

last updated: 2026-04-04

Why was this blog post created?

The SAP HANA database comes with a set of tools that support the work of database administrators, developers, and other support teams. The tools are available both internally (SAP Employees) and externally. Knowledge of this set of tools is essential for SAP HANA database operators.

Where can I find the most important information about SQL Statement Collection reports?

The following SAP Note is the central source of the toolkit 🔧 called "SQL Statement Collection".

📘 1969700 - SQL Statement Collection for SAP HANA

There are huge number of reports available for different purposes. There is a SAP Knowledge Base Article created to explain the reports one-by-one in details.

📕 3311408 - Bookmark of SQL Statement Collection reports for SAP HANA

The SAP Knowledge Base Article is under construction and will contain more reports in the future.

Other articles

✍️ Where can I find knowledge and information belongs to SAP HANA?
✍️ Where can I find information about the available tools for SAP HANA (all types of use)?

Do you have further questions?

Please do not hesitate to contact me if you have question or observation regarding the article.
Q&A link for SAP HANA: https://answers.sap.com/tags/73554900100700000996 

Contribution

If you find any missing information belongs to the topic, please let me know. I am happy to add the new content. My intention is to maintain the content continuously to keep the info up-to-date.

Release Information

Release DateDescription
2026.04.04First/initial Release of the SAP Blog Post documentation (Technical Article).

entry.title: Re: SAC Report is unable to display on webpage but able t...
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

Hope you are well. Thank you for your message.

For this story, whether it is now based on the Live Data Connection to Datasphere models? If yes, please kindly refer to the information in the following KBAs, which may provide some insights on the error message in this story:

  • 2729600 - The data cannot be displayed ... error when refreshing a story with many widgets and/or data in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) - SAP for Me
  • 3575377 - Max result record numbers when consumed by SAP HANA Multi-Dimensional Services (MDS) In Datasphere - SAP for Me

==>

Based on the information in the above KBAs, the appearance of this error message is due to the limitation in the underlying HANA databases belong to SAP Datasphere. The limitation is controlled by MDS parameters in HANA database as per SAP Note 2770570. Unfortunately, the parameters cannot be changed in the underlying HANA database belong to Datasphere. Reduce the table result data amount to avoid the error message. For example:

  • Reduce the number of dimensions on the axes.
  • Reduce the number of exception aggregation dimensions.
  • Reduce the number of measures/accounts requested.
  • Add more restrictive filters
    • for dimensions which are on an axis,
    • for dimensions which may, directly or indirectly, be used as an exception aggregation dimension.

Thank you for your time.

Best wishes,

Lena

entry.title: Re: Accessing and Calling Existing BRF+ Functions in SAP...
entry.content:
Dear @sebastian_otte,

In Public Cloud this should not be positioned as a supported way to reuse SAP standard BRF+ content from developer extensibility. SAP documents CL_FDT_FUNCTION_PROCESS_API as the released API to consume BRF+ rules in ABAP Cloud but SAP also documents that cloud development must rely on released objects available for USE_IN_CLOUD_DEVELOPMENT. Therefore, knowing or retrieving the technical ID of a standard BRF+ function is not the supported contract for custom code

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/sap-btp-abap-environment/abap-environment/api-for-consumption-of-business-rules

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c0c54048d35849128be8e872df5bea6d/b6fe6846e9c7488e8c7e6d4289918fe3.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2bba750d1e124e1ea2a039bb1cd9b6c5/eb381da44986470cadfa80891d81fe6d.htm

For the authorization part, SAP does document access to the Simplified BRF+ Workbench – Extensibility via the extensibility business role/catalog, for example SAP_BR_EXTENSIBILITY_SPEC and SAP_CORE_BC_EXT_FDT. However, that documents access to the workbench capability itself. SAP does not position SAP standard OPD BRF+ applications such as OPD WM Handling Unit as customer-released reuse objects for ABAP Cloud development. Because of that, even if the app/catalog is assigned this does not make the underlying SAP-delivered BRF+ functions a supported API for custom code

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-business-technology-platform/business-catalogs-and-business-roles

The supported workaround is to use one of these two patterns:

  1. Create your own customer BRF+ application/function and call it from your ABAP class via CL_FDT_FUNCTION_PROCESS_API. That is the SAP-documented BRF+ consumption pattern for ABAP Cloud
  2. If the business requirement is related to Handling Unit output / OPD logic, use the supported Output Parameter Determination extensibility instead of trying to call the underlying SAP standard OPD BRF+ function directly. SAP documents Output Parameter Determination for Handling Units the available condition parameters for that application type, and the possibility to extend HU processing with custom fields

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/32da8359c8ee4e8b8e8c5e15cacba5aa/36597ffd10024278bdb455c22bfd8c60.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/32da8359c8ee4e8b8e8c5e15cacba5aa/50d5a1785ab049aab89b865964538689.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/32da8359c8ee4e8b8e8c5e15cacba5aa/c4958e6cec764e8882a37c4e2e9fdb7f.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/4aea5a6d73744ea2ae66430230ebcba0.html

Summary:

In Public Cloud access to the BRF+ workbench does not make SAP standard OPD BRF+ objects a supported developer API. The clean-core solution is to use your own BRF+ function via CL_FDT_FUNCTION_PROCESS_API or a released HU/OPD extension mechanism explicitly supported by SAP

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: Building/Representing Chemical Processes in SAP (Inpu...
entry.content:
Hello

regarding

Can someone direct me to information on building out a chemical process in SAP from initial raw materials with specific CAS#'s and chemical propertied into finished goods with different CAS#'s and chemical properties? An example would be the capture of ammonia gas by sulfuric acid to generate ammonia sulfate and water.

I assume you are not using "Recipe Development" or "Recipe Management"

Reading between the lines:

1:) what process are you looking for ? Are we talking about SAP PP process?

2.) High level this is true

a.) in SAP (either R/3 or S/4) most of the process are material oriented (e.g. SAP MM, SAP SD, SAP PP and similar stuff)

b.) so therefore it is high level simple: any chemical process need (most of the cases) a SAP PP process

And here it is quite simple: You have a "target" material and you need source materials  to use SAP PP

In your example:

ammonia gas by sulfuric acid to generate ammonia sulfate and water.

The "ammonia sulfate" / "Water" are "Target"s.  In most of the cases: the result is a solution of "ammonia sulfate in water" which you can define by using a materal number

Theerfore: sulfric acid (is in most of the cases) a solved in Water.

Therefore: the ammonia gas can be represented by a material number and the sulfric acid the same

So quite simple.. or are you looking for something different?

C.B.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud - Configure Value Mapping :...
entry.content:
Dear @hans_segers,

SAP does not document the values maintained in Configure Value Mapping as a released standard CDS view or a released standard OData API for ABAP Cloud consumption. The app is documented as SAP GUI for HTML transaction and SAP documentation places /AIF/VMAP_CONF and /AIF/VMAP in the AIF value-mapping area rather than documenting a released API contract for custom development. In ABAP Cloud, custom code may consume only released APIs. SAP also documents that custom CDS views can be created only on top of a released SAP VDM view or an existing custom CDS view. Based on that release model this should not be positioned as a supported CDS/OData source unless SAP explicitly releases such an object

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/ABAP_PLATFORM_NEW/4db1676c3f114f119b500bd80ccd944d/ca6410109b014c6c97f58c4f34dd6662.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/ABAP_PLATFORM_NEW/4db1676c3f114f119b500bd80ccd944d/2f281e2bd10746328e151e9b044dd25e.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/b0000072fe994d7b9d5e015adf066059.html

https://help.sap.com/doc/abapdocu_cp_index_htm/CLOUD/en-US/ABENABAP_LANGUAGE_VERSIONS.html

https://help.sap.com/doc/abapdocu_cp_index_htm/CLOUD/en-US/ABENABAP_API_RELEASE.html

Summary:

  • CDS: SAP does not document a released standard CDS source for these value mappings
  • OData: SAP does not document a released standard OData API for these value mappings
  • ABAP Cloud: the supported validation point is the Released Objects view in ADT if no released object/service exists there for this purpose, it is not a supported ABAP Cloud contract

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/abap-cloud/abap-development-tools-user-guide/finding-released-apis-and-deprecated-objects

Best Regards,

Dawid

1. Introduction to ABAP Cloud supported by generative AI | BTP1429: Get an overview on ABAP Cloud and recent innovations, get insights on Clean Core and see how generative AI boosts your adoption of ABAP Cloud conducted by Alexander Rother (VP, Head of ABAP Platform Product Management).

2. Build smarter: Agentic ABAP development tools for Visual Studio Code | BTP2573: Simona Marincei (Head of AI - SAP ABAP Platform) will show how ABAP agents orchestrate in VS Code via the ABAP MCP-server tasks, speed up coding, and boost productivity across orchestrators from various partners.

3. Agentic ABAP with SAP Joule for Developers: Build faster, migrate smarter | JOU1428: Simona Marincei (Head of AI - SAP ABAP Platform) and Nora Klemp (Senior Product Specialist) will explain how agentic ABAP accelerates SAP S/4HANA migrations and streamline ABAP Cloud development across modern and legacy environments.

4. Customer Influence and feedback: The product road map for ABAP Cloud | BTP1603: Learn from Alexander Rother (VP, Head of ABAP Platform Product Management) how to give feedback to the ABAP product roadmap with the Customer Influence site which allows submitting, tracking, and prioritizing ideas.

5. Modernize classic extensions for clean core in SAP Cloud ERP Private | BTP1426: Volker Drees (Product Expert, ABAP Cloud, SAP SE) will explain clean core extensibility practice in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. Learn how to govern clean core development using the ABAP test cockpit and how to use the ABAP Cloud development model and clean core extensibility principles to modernize your custom on-stack extensions.

entry.title: Re: K6 Load Testing for SAP BTP (CAP + Fiori): Scope, CI/...
entry.content:
Dear @theodore_abitbol,

Question 1.
From an SAP BTP architecture perspective, k6 is most relevant for service- and integration-level performance testing of CAP, OData and API endpoints, either called directly or through the application router when authentication, routing and destination behavior are part of scope. Since SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition runs on the managed application router, it is reasonable to keep Work Zone in scope only for a small number of critical end-to-end journeys, while using backend/API tests as the main performance benchmark

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-business-technology-platform/managed-application-router?version=Cloud

https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-business-technology-platform/application-router-configuration

https://help.sap.com/docs/build-work-zone-standard-edition/sap-build-work-zone-standard-edition/expose-html5-applications-in-sap-build-work-zone-standard-edition

Question 2.
Yes. From a delivery perspective, it is reasonable to run k6 after deployment to a test or performance environment, because that is where the real SAP BTP runtime path exists, including the application router and related service integrations. For pipeline control, k6 supports thresholds as pass/fail criteria so the practical pattern is build -> deploy -> smoke test -> performance test

More details below:

https://grafana.com/docs/k6/latest/using-k6/thresholds/

https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-business-technology-platform/application-router-configuration

Question 3.
For CAP/OData services the safer recommendation is to start with backend throughput and concurrent service demand, rather than treating full UI-style user simulation as the primary benchmark. Virtual-user-style scenarios still make sense for stateful business flows but for this type of SAP BTP application the main technical question is usually how the service layer behaves under sustained parallel load

More details below:

https://grafana.com/docs/k6/latest/using-k6/protocols/

Question 4.
A single reused OAuth token can be acceptable for pure technical load tests but it should not be treated as the default where authorization, role collections, or user propagation are relevant. SAP clearly distinguishes OAuth2ClientCredentials from OAuth2UserTokenExchange so where business behavior depends on real user context the more defensible setup is a small pool of dedicated test users; for write requests through the application router, CSRF handling should also be included

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/doc/92c0b171cceb4db2a93518b34b84febd/Cloud/en-US/connectivity_service.pdf

Summary:

For an MTA application deployed on SAP Build Work Zone the most defensible position is to treat k6 primarily as a backend/API performance testing tool with only a limited Work Zone end-to-end layer for selected journeys. From the SAP BTP perspective the strongest default is to run performance tests after deployment, focus mainly on the service/integration layer and use real user pools only where user context materially affects runtime behavior

Best Regards,

Dawid
